<!-- Generated by sourcery-ai[bot]: start review_guide -->

## Reviewer's Guide

Implements centralized client API helpers, adds Gmail inbox/summarization/chat capabilities with PKCE storage backed by Redis, introduces a mails UI with WebSocket-driven notifications, enhances agent chat to auto-create tasks from chat commands, and refactors task and Gmail APIs for safer, more consistent behavior and responses.

#### Sequence diagram for Gmail email summarization and chat

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  actor User
  participant MailsPage
  participant ApiHelper
  participant EmailRouter
  participant DB
  participant GeminiAPI

  User->>MailsPage: Select email
  MailsPage->>ApiHelper: apiFetch(/gmail/emails)
  ApiHelper->>EmailRouter: GET /gmail/emails (auth)
  EmailRouter->>DB: query Email by user_id
  DB-->>EmailRouter: list Email
  EmailRouter-->>ApiHelper: { total, items }
  ApiHelper-->>MailsPage: emails JSON
  MailsPage->>User: Render email list

  User->>MailsPage: Click Summarize this mail
  MailsPage->>ApiHelper: apiFetch(/gmail/emails/{id}/summarize, POST)
  ApiHelper->>EmailRouter: POST /gmail/emails/{id}/summarize
  EmailRouter->>DB: load Email(id, user_id)
  DB-->>EmailRouter: Email
  EmailRouter->>GeminiAPI: generate_content(prompt with subject and body)
  GeminiAPI-->>EmailRouter: summary_text
  EmailRouter->>DB: update Email.summary
  DB-->>EmailRouter: commit
  EmailRouter-->>ApiHelper: { summary }
  ApiHelper-->>MailsPage: summary JSON
  MailsPage->>MailsPage: update selected.summary
  MailsPage->>ApiHelper: apiFetch(/gmail/emails) to refresh
  ApiHelper->>EmailRouter: GET /gmail/emails
  EmailRouter->>DB: query Email
  DB-->>EmailRouter: list Email
  EmailRouter-->>ApiHelper: { total, items }
  ApiHelper-->>MailsPage: updated emails
  MailsPage->>User: Show summary and refreshed list

  User->>MailsPage: Ask question about email
  MailsPage->>ApiHelper: apiFetch(/gmail/emails/{id}/chat, POST message)
  ApiHelper->>EmailRouter: POST /gmail/emails/{id}/chat
  EmailRouter->>DB: load Email(id, user_id)
  DB-->>EmailRouter: Email
  EmailRouter->>GeminiAPI: generate_content(prompt with email and message)
  GeminiAPI-->>EmailRouter: reply_text
  EmailRouter-->>ApiHelper: { reply }
  ApiHelper-->>MailsPage: reply JSON
  MailsPage->>MailsPage: append AI reply to chat
  MailsPage->>User: Show assistant answer
```

#### Sequence diagram for agent chat with auto task creation

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  actor User
  participant ChatPage
  participant ApiHelper
  participant AgentRouter
  participant DB
  participant PlannerAgent

  User->>ChatPage: Submit message (e.g. add task: Submit report)
  ChatPage->>ApiHelper: apiFetch(/agent/chat, POST message)
  ApiHelper->>AgentRouter: POST /agent/chat (ChatRequest)
  AgentRouter->>AgentRouter: _try_create_task_from_message(message)
  AgentRouter->>DB: insert Task(user_id, title, is_ai_generated)
  DB-->>AgentRouter: commit
  AgentRouter->>PlannerAgent: run_planner_agent(user_id, message, db)
  PlannerAgent-->>AgentRouter: reply_text
  AgentRouter->>AgentRouter: prepend confirmation if task_created
  AgentRouter-->>ApiHelper: ChatResponse(reply, task_created=True)
  ApiHelper-->>ChatPage: ChatResponse JSON
  ChatPage->>ChatPage: append AI message to chat
  ChatPage->>User: Show reply with implicit task creation
```

#### Sequence diagram for WebSocket email processed notifications and mail refresh

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant WebSocketServer
  participant Layout
  participant Window
  participant MailsPage
  participant User
  participant EmailSyncProcess
  participant Backend

  WebSocketServer-->>Layout: MESSAGE { type: EMAIL_PROCESSED }
  Layout->>Layout: onmessage handler
  Layout->>Layout: setHasMailNotification(true)

  User->>Layout: Click Mails nav item
  Layout->>Layout: setHasMailNotification(false)
  Layout->>MailsPage: render route /mails

  EmailSyncProcess-->>WebSocketServer: EMAIL_PROCESSED event
  WebSocketServer-->>Layout: MESSAGE { type: EMAIL_PROCESSED }
  Layout->>Window: dispatchEvent(CustomEvent app:live-update)

  Window-->>MailsPage: app:live-update event
  MailsPage->>MailsPage: fetchEmails()
  MailsPage->>Backend: GET /gmail/emails via apiFetch
  Backend-->>MailsPage: latest emails
  MailsPage->>User: Updated email list
```

#### Entity relationship diagram for users, emails, and tasks

```mermaid
erDiagram
  User {
    int id
    string email
    string gmail_access_token
    string gmail_refresh_token
    datetime gmail_token_expiry
    bool gmail_connected
  }

  Email {
    int id
    int user_id
    string subject
    string sender
    text body
    text summary
    datetime created_at
  }

  Task {
    int id
    int user_id
    string title
    text description
    string priority
    string status
    datetime due_date
    bool is_ai_generated
  }

  User ||--o{ Email : owns
  User ||--o{ Task : owns
```

#### Class diagram for updated email, agent, and task APIs

```mermaid
classDiagram
  class EmailRouter {
    +connect_gmail(current_user)
    +gmail_callback(code, state, db)
    +sync_gmail(db, current_user)
    +list_emails(db, current_user)
    +summarize_email(email_id, db, current_user)
    +chat_about_email(email_id, req, db, current_user)
    +gmail_status(current_user)
    +disconnect_gmail(db, current_user)
    -_generate_pkce()
    -_pkce_key(user_id)
    -_save_code_verifier(user_id, verifier)
    -_pop_code_verifier(user_id)
  }

  class AgentRouter {
    +chat(req, db, current_user) ChatResponse
    -_try_create_task_from_message(message, user_id, db) bool
  }

  class TaskRouter {
    +create_task(data, db, user) TaskResponse
    +get_all_tasks(status, priority, search, db, user) TaskListResponse
    +update_task(task_id, data, user, db) TaskResponse
    +delete_task(task_id, db, user)
  }

  class EmailChatRequest {
    +string message
  }

  class ChatRequest {
    +string message
  }

  class ChatResponse {
    +string reply
    +bool task_created
  }

  class TaskCreate {
    +string title
    +string description
    +TaskPriority priority
    +datetime due_date
  }

  class TaskUpdate {
    +string title
    +string description
    +TaskPriority priority
    +TaskStatus status
    +datetime due_date
  }

  class TaskResponse {
    +int id
    +string title
    +string description
    +TaskPriority priority
    +TaskStatus status
    +datetime due_date
  }

  class TaskListResponse {
    +int total
    +TaskResponse[] items
  }

  class TaskPriority {
    <<enum>>
  }

  class TaskStatus {
    <<enum>>
  }

  class Task {
    +int id
    +int user_id
    +string title
    +string description
    +TaskPriority priority
    +TaskStatus status
    +datetime due_date
    +bool is_ai_generated
  }

  class Email {
    +int id
    +int user_id
    +string subject
    +string sender
    +string body
    +string summary
    +datetime created_at
  }

  class User {
    +int id
    +string email
    +string gmail_access_token
    +string gmail_refresh_token
    +datetime gmail_token_expiry
    +bool gmail_connected
  }

  EmailRouter --> EmailChatRequest
  AgentRouter --> ChatRequest
  AgentRouter --> ChatResponse
  AgentRouter --> Task
  TaskRouter --> TaskCreate
  TaskRouter --> TaskUpdate
  TaskRouter --> TaskResponse
  TaskRouter --> TaskListResponse
  TaskResponse --> TaskPriority
  TaskResponse --> TaskStatus
  Task --> TaskPriority
  Task --> TaskStatus
  Email --> User
  Task --> User
```

### File-Level Changes

| Change | Details | Files |
| ------ | ------- | ----- |
| Centralize client API and WebSocket URL construction and migrate existing fetch calls. | <ul><li>Added api.js with API_BASE_URL, apiFetch helper, and buildWebSocketUrl for protocol-aware WebSocket URLs.</li><li>Replaced hardcoded http://localhost:8000 fetch calls across App, Overview, TasksPage, ChatPage, and SettingsPage with apiFetch.</li><li>Updated Layout to construct WebSocket connections via buildWebSocketUrl instead of hardcoded ws://localhost:8000.</li></ul> | `client/src/api.js`<br/>`client/src/App.jsx`<br/>`client/src/pages/Overview.jsx`<br/>`client/src/pages/TasksPage.jsx`<br/>`client/src/pages/ChatPage.jsx`<br/>`client/src/pages/SettingsPage.jsx`<br/>`client/src/components/Layout.jsx` |
| Add Gmail inbox browsing, summarization, and email-centric chat endpoints and UI. | <ul><li>Extended Gmail API router to use Redis-backed PKCE storage with in-memory fallback for OAuth code verifiers.</li><li>Added /gmail/emails listing endpoint backed by Email model and ordered by created_at, plus /gmail/emails/{id}/summarize and /gmail/emails/{id}/chat routes using Gemini via google.generativeai.</li><li>Exposed /gmail/sync to run process_user_emails directly and adjusted callback error handling and disconnect behavior.</li><li>Created MailsPage UI to list emails, view details, trigger summarization, and chat about a selected email, including listening for app:live-update events.</li><li>Wired a new /mails route into the app and navigation with a notification badge driven by WebSocket EMAIL_PROCESSED messages.</li></ul> | `server/app/api/email.py`<br/>`client/src/pages/MailsPage.jsx`<br/>`client/src/App.jsx`<br/>`client/src/components/Layout.jsx` |
| Improve task API robustness and client UX for task operations. | <ul><li>Refactored task API imports and type usage to rely on schema enums for TaskStatus and TaskPriority and removed duplicate get_all_tasks definition.</li><li>Updated task listing to accept optional status, priority, and search query parameters with consistent TaskListResponse payload shape.</li><li>Hardened update_task to treat optional fields correctly (checking for None) and simplified status/priority validation by trusting Pydantic enums.</li><li>Kept delete_task and create_task behavior but standardized signatures, spacing, and error messages.</li><li>Enhanced TasksPage with loading and error states and migrated to apiFetch for all task operations.</li></ul> | `server/app/api/tasks.py`<br/>`client/src/pages/TasksPage.jsx` |
| Enhance agent chat to support task creation via chat commands and expose this in the schema and UI. | <ul><li>Added ChatResponse.task_created field in Pydantic schema with default False.</li><li>Introduced helper _try_create_task_from_message in agent API to parse "add/create task: <title>" commands via regex and conditionally create Task records marked is_ai_generated.</li><li>Modified /agent/chat to invoke the helper, wrap the agent reply with a confirmation prefix when a task is created, and return task_created in the API response.</li><li>Updated ChatPage to use apiFetch and surface inline network error messaging plus a tip describing the add task: pattern.</li></ul> | `server/app/api/agent.py`<br/>`server/app/schemas/agent.py`<br/>`client/src/pages/ChatPage.jsx` |
| Improve Gmail integration UX and status handling on the client side. | <ul><li>Updated SettingsPage to use apiFetch for Gmail status, connect, disconnect, and sync operations and added a statusMessage banner for user feedback.</li><li>On successful sync, dispatches a global app:live-update event with SYNC_COMPLETE so other views (e.g., MailsPage) can refresh.</li><li>Refined error handling for failed connect/disconnect/sync/status operations with user-visible messages instead of silent failures.</li></ul> | `client/src/pages/SettingsPage.jsx` |
| Add repository hygiene configuration. | <ul><li>Introduced a .gitignore with common Python, Node, and build artifacts to prevent committing generated files.</li></ul> | `.gitignore` |

---

<details>
<summary>Tips and commands</summary>

#### Interacting with Sourcery

- **Trigger a new review:** Comment `@sourcery-ai review` on the pull request.
- **Continue discussions:** Reply directly to Sourcery's review comments.
- **Generate a GitHub issue from a review comment:** Ask Sourcery to create an
  issue from a review comment by replying to it. You can also reply to a
  review comment with `@sourcery-ai issue` to create an issue from it.
- **Generate a pull request title:** Write `@sourcery-ai` anywhere in the pull
  request title to generate a title at any time. You can also comment
  `@sourcery-ai title` on the pull request to (re-)generate the title at any time.
- **Generate a pull request summary:** Write `@sourcery-ai summary` anywhere in
  the pull request body to generate a PR summary at any time exactly where you
  want it. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai summary` on the pull request to
  (re-)generate the summary at any time.
- **Generate reviewer's guide:** Comment `@sourcery-ai guide` on the pull
  request to (re-)generate the reviewer's guide at any time.
- **Resolve all Sourcery comments:** Comment `@sourcery-ai resolve` on the
  pull request to resolve all Sourcery comments. Useful if you've already
  addressed all the comments and don't want to see them anymore.
- **Dismiss all Sourcery reviews:** Comment `@sourcery-ai dismiss` on the pull
  request to dismiss all existing Sourcery reviews. Especially useful if you
  want to start fresh with a new review - don't forget to comment
  `@sourcery-ai review` to trigger a new review!

#### Customizing Your Experience

Access your [dashboard](https://app.sourcery.ai) to:
- Enable or disable review features such as the Sourcery-generated pull request
  summary, the reviewer's guide, and others.
- Change the review language.
- Add, remove or edit custom review instructions.
- Adjust other review settings.

#### Getting Help

- [Contact our support team](mailto:support@sourcery.ai) for questions or feedback.
- Visit our [documentation](https://docs.sourcery.ai) for detailed guides and information.
- Keep in touch with the Sourcery team by following us on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/SourceryAI), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/sourcery-ai/) or [GitHub](https://github.com/sourcery-ai).

</details>
